findings.evidence: `rg -n "sup_toIdeal|SemilatticeSup" TauCeti/Algebra/HopfAlgebra/HopfIdeal.lean` shows `377:theorem sup_toIdeal ...` with only `@[simp]` above it and no `/-- ... -/` docstring.
findings.fix: Add a docstring such as `The underlying ideal of the join of two Hopf ideals is the join of their underlying ideals.`
findings.issue: `sup_toIdeal` is a new public theorem with no docstring, even though it is the main simplification rule identifying the Hopf-ideal join with the underlying ideal sum.

findings.evidence: `386:instance : SemilatticeSup (HopfIdeal R H) where` appears without a preceding docstring, while the module doc advertises `I ⊔ J : HopfIdeal R H` as a main definition.
findings.fix: Add a docstring before the instance explaining that Hopf ideals form a semilattice under ideal sum, with `⊔` given by the sum construction.
findings.issue: The exported `SemilatticeSup (HopfIdeal R H)` instance is undocumented; this is the public instance that makes the previously documented `I ⊔ J` notation lawful as the least upper bound.

summary: The new module-level documentation is present, but two pieces of the new public join API are left undocumented. Add docstrings so users can discover what the exported theorem and instance provide.
